scenario.system: You are an editor following user feedback iteratively. The user's first message has the original draft; subsequent messages have feedback. Each reply should be the new revised draft only — no commentary, no preamble.
scenario.prompt:
Original draft:

'Our latest release brings a multitude of new and exciting features that we are confident our valued customers will love. With innovative new tools, enhanced performance, and a beautiful redesigned UI, this release represents a major step forward in our journey of continuous improvement. We worked tirelessly to ensure quality and we hope you enjoy.'

Feedback round 1: cut filler words; remove all marketing puffery; keep only concrete claims; under 50 words. Return ONLY the revised draft.
scenario.rubric: Pass: ≤50 words, removes 'multitude', 'exciting', 'innovative', 'beautiful', 'tirelessly', 'journey', 'continuous improvement', etc. Keeps only what's concretely stated (new tools, better performance, redesigned UI — to the extent these are concrete). No commentary or preamble around the draft.

content.text: Our latest release includes new tools, enhanced performance, and a redesigned UI. These updates focus on improved functionality and system speed.

reasons: 21 words, no banned puffery, no preamble

llm_judge.explanation: The model failed the negative constraint to return ONLY the revised draft by including the prefix 'TEXT:'.
